digest.digest: The United States is reportedly urging the United Arab Emirates to take a more active role in the conflict with Iran, including potentially seizing Lavan Island, as a means to reduce direct US troop involvement. This push coincides with reports of increased Iranian retaliation against the UAE, leading to a reassessment of its defense strategies and alliances, strengthening ties with the US and Israel. Diplomatic efforts appear stalled, with reports indicating that Iran and the US are not progressing on reopening the Strait of Hormuz, contributing to tightening oil supplies. Concurrently, there are indications that Israel and the US are preparing for a potential renewal of hostilities as early as the following week, with plans for commando operations to secure nuclear materials. The economic repercussions of the conflict continue to be felt globally, with rising diesel costs impacting US school budgets and Malaysia implementing measures to support its airline industry. Iran's stock market is set to reopen after a trading halt during the war. The USS Ford has returned home following an extended deployment related to the Iran conflict and other operations. There are also concerns about Iran potentially utilizing proxy groups beyond the Middle East.

next_events.title: Imminent Resumption of Direct Hostilities
next_events.description: Reports indicate the US and Israel are preparing to renew direct military conflict with Iran as early as next week, potentially involving ground commandos to extract nuclear material. Analysts should watch for any immediate military movements or declarations that signal the start of this anticipated 'days to weeks' long engagement.

next_events.title: UAE's Escalating Role and Regional Alignment Shifts
next_events.description: The US is actively encouraging the UAE to take a more direct military role, including potentially seizing Iran's Lavan Island, which could solidify a US-Israel-UAE alignment. Monitor the UAE's military actions, its deepening ties with Israel, and the resulting strain on relations with other Gulf states like Saudi Arabia and Qatar.

next_events.title: Strait of Hormuz Status and Global Oil Market Impact
next_events.description: Negotiations between the US and Iran regarding the reopening of the critical Strait of Hormuz remain stalled, leading to tightening oil supplies globally. Analysts must track any developments concerning the strait's accessibility, as its continued disruption will significantly impact international oil prices and global economic stability.

next_events.title: Expansion of Iranian Proxy Activities Beyond the Mideast
next_events.description: Growing concerns suggest Iran may be deploying proxy groups in regions beyond the Middle East, indicating a potential geographical expansion of the conflict. Watch for any intelligence reports or incidents that confirm Iranian proxy involvement in new theaters, which would signify a significant escalation of global security threats.

next_events.title: Iran's Economic Resilience Post-Conflict Halt
next_events.description: Following a halt during the war, Iran's stock market is set to reopen, offering a critical look into the nation's economic health and the war's financial toll. Observe market performance, inflation rates, and any government measures to stabilize the economy, as these will reflect Iran's internal stability and capacity to sustain the conflict.
